questions: Q1: Does the resolved tickets/index.ts preserve both ticket and staging intents?
Q2: Are there any technical changes required for the CLI repo beyond build verification?

answers.answer: Yes. The resolved tickets/index.ts (150 lines) has all 10 subcommand handler imports (list, latest, get, create, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, bundle, update-description) and the standard switch-based dispatcher. Both ticket's enhancements and staging's changes coexist. Zero conflict markers found.
evidence: tickets/index.ts lines 43-148: complete switch dispatcher with all subcommands

answers.answer: No. All 6 library CLI command files exist in src/library/ and are separate from the conflict. Resolution utility exists at src/lib/resolve-library-item.ts. Main dispatcher has library case at src/index.ts lines 98-102. SKILL.md has Library section at lines 146-179. Only npm run build (tsc) verification needed.
evidence: merge-conflicts.json lists ONLY tickets/index.ts — library files unaffected
scout/reference-map.json: all 6 library files + resolve-library-item.ts present
scout/scout-summary.md: main entry src/index.ts has library case

statement: The CLI repo's single conflict (src/tickets/index.ts) is pre-resolved with zero markers. The file is the ticket subcommand dispatcher — unrelated to the library module. Both ticket and staging intents are preserved. All library CLI commands in src/library/ are intact. No code changes required — build verification only.
